prose:
The primary efficacy analysis evaluated change from baseline in glycated haemoglobin (HbA1c) at Week 24 using a Mixed Model for Repeated Measures (MMRM) in the Full Analysis Set (FAS). Both doses of STR-4021 demonstrated statistically significant and clinically meaningful reductions in HbA1c compared with placebo at Week 24.

The least squares (LS) mean change from baseline in HbA1c at Week 24 was −0.82% (95% CI: −1.04, −0.60) for STR-4021 10 mg, −1.14% (95% CI: −1.36, −0.92) for STR-4021 25 mg, and −0.11% (95% CI: −0.33, +0.11) for placebo. The LS mean treatment differences versus placebo were −0.71% (95% CI: −1.02, −0.40; p<0.001) for STR-4021 10 mg and −1.03% (95% CI: −1.34, −0.72; p<0.001) for STR-4021 25 mg.

Both treatment differences exceeded the pre-specified clinically meaningful threshold of 0.5% reduction in HbA1c. The results were robust across all pre-specified sensitivity analyses, including the per-protocol population and pattern mixture model analyses addressing potential missing-not-at-random departures.
